ground_truth_summary: Six brain MRI slices in a 2x3 grid showing coronal/sagittal/axial planes. Orientation markers R, MF, and AL (AL only on bottom-middle). 5cm scale on all panels EXCEPT the middle column. NO annotations, arrows, or visible Parkinson's-specific lesions.

judgments.hallucination_details: No fabricated lesions or annotations claimed. Anatomical descriptions are reasonable. Minor: scale-bar placement and MF/AL positions described loosely but not materially wrong.
judgments.accuracy: Correctly identifies 2x3 grid, 6 MRI slices, planes (coronal/sagittal/axial). Correctly notes AL on bottom-middle panel, MF present, R marker present. Correctly identifies 5cm scale bars. No hallucinated Parkinson's features or lesions.
errors: Claims '5cm' appears on top-middle panel scale, but ground truth says middle column has NO 5cm scale.
Claims '5cm' appears on bottom-middle panel, which ground truth states is in the middle column (no scale).
Slight imprecision on which exact panels have which marker placement.
judgments.completeness: Comprehensive coverage of layout, text, panels, colors, and features.
judgments.ranking_rationale: Excellent description overall; only minor errors on scale bar presence in middle column. No hallucination.

judgments.hallucination_details: MAJOR HALLUCINATION: Claims 'A highly notable feature is present in the bottom middle panel (sagittal view). This panel clearly shows a large, well-defined mass or lesion located in the posterior fossa, specifically involving the cerebellum. This mass is significantly larger than the surrounding normal tissue.' This is fabricated — the ground truth explicitly states no lesions or Parkinson's-specific features are visible.
judgments.accuracy: Correctly identifies 2x3 grid and orientation markers. INCORRECTLY claims T2-weighted (ground truth notes likely T1). Hallucinates a large cerebellar mass/lesion.
errors: HALLUCINATION: Fabricated large mass/lesion in posterior fossa/cerebellum in bottom-middle panel.
Claims T2-weighted sequence — not verified, possibly wrong.
Inconsistent view identification (e.g., says bottom-left is both axial and coronal in different places).
judgments.completeness: Covers layout and text, but the hallucinated pathology dominates the response.
judgments.ranking_rationale: Disqualified by significant hallucination of a brain lesion that does not exist. This is exactly the kind of fabricated finding the prompt warns against.

errors: Claims 5cm appears on bottom-middle panel which ground truth says is in middle column (no scale).
Claims 5cm on top-middle which is also middle column (no scale).
Decodes AL as 'axial level' — speculative but not a hallucination of a feature.
judgments.completeness: Good coverage of layout, text, objects, colors, and features.
judgments.ranking_rationale: Solid description with no hallucination; minor errors on middle-column scale bars.
